description: Enhanced status command showing PID, uptime, last poll, repos watched, DB size, today's events — answer 'is it working?' without reading logs

userStories.title: DB: last_poll_at timestamp table
userStories.description: Add a `daemon_state` key-value table to persist daemon heartbeat data. The daemon writes a `last_poll_at` timestamp (RFC3339) after every full_scan() call in poller.rs. This is the only source of truth for when the daemon last polled; the status command reads it without needing to communicate with the daemon process directly.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration in db.rs `open_db()` migrations array: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS daemon_state (key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT NOT NULL);`
`pub fn set_daemon_state(conn: &Connection, key: &str, value: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()>` in db.rs — uses INSERT OR REPLACE
`pub fn get_daemon_state(conn: &Connection, key: &str) -> anyhow::Result<Option<String>>` in db.rs — returns None if key absent
Both functions are `pub` and registered via standard db.rs pattern
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: Poller: write last_poll_at after every full_scan
userStories.description: After each full_scan() call in run_poll_loop(), write `last_poll_at` = current UTC RFC3339 timestamp to the daemon_state table. Also write `repos_watched` = count of repos as a string. This data is used by the status command.
acceptanceCriteria: After every call to `full_scan()` in run_poll_loop() (both watcher and polling paths), call `db::set_daemon_state(&conn, "last_poll_at", &now_rfc3339)` where now is chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339()
After every full_scan(), call `db::set_daemon_state(&conn, "repos_watched", &repos.len().to_string())`
Both writes are fire-and-forget (log warn on error, don't propagate)
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: daemon.rs: DaemonStatus struct and get_daemon_status()
userStories.description: Add a `DaemonStatus` struct and a `get_daemon_status()` function in daemon.rs that collects all fields needed by the status command. The function is the single source of truth for status data — both pretty and JSON rendering call it.
acceptanceCriteria: `pub struct DaemonStatus` in daemon.rs with fields: `running: bool`, `pid: Option<u32>`, `uptime_secs: Option<u64>`, `last_poll_at: Option<chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>>`, `repos_watched: Option<u64>`, `db_size_bytes: Option<u64>`, `events_today: Option<u64>`, `health: HealthIndicator`
`pub enum HealthIndicator { Green, Yellow, Red }` in daemon.rs — derived Debug, serde::Serialize

Health rules: Green = daemon running AND last_poll_at within 5 minutes; Yellow = daemon running AND last_poll_at between 5–30 minutes ago (or last_poll_at unknown); Red = daemon not running OR last_poll_at older than 30 minutes

`pub fn get_daemon_status(data_dir: &Path) -> anyhow::Result<DaemonStatus>` — reads PID via is_daemon_running(), reads daemon_state from DB (open_db gracefully if DB doesn't exist), reads db file size via std::fs::metadata, queries events_today count via SQL
uptime_secs: read PID file mtime via std::fs::metadata(&pid_file_path).modified() → elapsed seconds since then; None if daemon not running
events_today: `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM git_activity WHERE timestamp >= ?1` where ?1 = start of today UTC (midnight); None if DB doesn't exist
If DB doesn't exist: db_size_bytes=None, last_poll_at=None, repos_watched=None, events_today=None — no error
DaemonStatus derives serde::Serialize for JSON output
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: daemon.rs: update daemon_status() to use DaemonStatus
userStories.description: Replace the existing `daemon_status()` function body to call `get_daemon_status()` and render pretty output. Add `--format` flag support via a new parameter. The CLI `Status` command gains an optional `--format` arg matching the OutputFormat enum used by Today/Week/Month.
acceptanceCriteria: `Commands::Status` in cli.rs gains `#[arg(long, default_value = "pretty")] format: OutputFormat`
`daemon_status(data_dir, format)` signature updated to accept `format: OutputFormat`
main.rs match arm for Commands::Status updated to pass format
Pretty output (see US-005 for exact format)
JSON output: `println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&status)?)` — DaemonStatus must implement serde::Serialize
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: Pretty status output with health indicator
userStories.description: Render a human-readable status block using `colored` crate for the health indicator. The output must answer 'is it working?' at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Status line 1: health icon + status text. Green=`✓ Running`, Yellow=`⚠ Running (stale)`, Red=`✗ Stopped`. Use `colored` crate: green.bold(), yellow.bold(), red.bold()
If running: `  PID:          <pid>`
If running: `  Uptime:       <Xh Ym Zs>` — format as hours/minutes/seconds, omit zero leading units (e.g. '5m 3s', '2h 1m')
Last poll: `  Last poll:    <X minutes ago>` or `  Last poll:    never` if None
`  Repos watched: <N>` or `  Repos watched: unknown` if None
`  DB size:       <X KB>` or `  DB size:       no DB yet` — format as KB (round to 1 decimal)
`  Events today:  <N>` or `  Events today:  0`
When daemon is stopped: only show Last poll, Repos watched, DB size, Events today (no PID/Uptime lines)
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: DB: events_today query helper
userStories.description: Add a dedicated query function to db.rs for counting today's git events. Keeps the query logic out of daemon.rs.
acceptanceCriteria: `pub fn count_events_today(conn: &Connection) -> anyhow::Result<u64>` in db.rs
SQL: `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM git_activity WHERE timestamp >= ?1` where ?1 = today's midnight UTC as RFC3339 (e.g. `2024-03-15T00:00:00+00:00`)
Returns 0 if table is empty
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: Unit tests: DaemonStatus struct and get_daemon_status()
userStories.description: Tests in tests/daemon_test.rs (or existing test file if one exists) covering the key states: daemon not running, stale PID, running daemon, fresh install with no DB.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: empty temp dir (no PID file, no DB) → DaemonStatus { running: false, pid: None, health: Red, db_size_bytes: None, events_today: None }
Test: temp dir with DB but no PID file → running=false, db_size_bytes=Some(N) where N > 0, events_today=Some(0)
Test: stale PID file (write a PID that doesn't exist) → running=false (is_daemon_running cleans up stale), health=Red
Test: DB with git_activity rows inserted today → events_today=Some(N) matches inserted count
Test: last_poll_at within 2 minutes → health=Green
Test: last_poll_at 10 minutes ago → health=Yellow
Test: last_poll_at 45 minutes ago → health=Red
Test: daemon_state table absent (old DB without migration) → last_poll_at=None, repos_watched=None — no panic
All tests pass

userStories.title: Unit tests: count_events_today()
userStories.description: Tests in tests/db_test.rs (or new tests/db_test.rs) for the count_events_today function.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: empty git_activity table → count_events_today returns Ok(0)
Test: one row with timestamp = now UTC → count_events_today returns Ok(1)
Test: one row with timestamp = yesterday UTC → count_events_today returns Ok(0)
Test: multiple rows, mix of today and yesterday → only today rows counted
All tests pass

userStories.title: Unit tests: set/get daemon_state
userStories.description: Tests for the daemon_state key-value store in db.rs.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: get_daemon_state on missing key returns Ok(None)
Test: set then get returns the set value
Test: set same key twice → second value wins (INSERT OR REPLACE)
All tests pass

userStories.title: Integration test: blackbox status pretty output
userStories.description: CLI integration test using assert_cmd. Run `blackbox status` against a temp environment with no daemon running and verify key output tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: blackbox status with no DB → output contains 'Stopped' or '✗'
Test: blackbox status --format json → output is valid JSON with keys: running, pid, health, events_today
Test: blackbox status --format json with no daemon → `running: false`, `health: 'Red'`
All tests pass

userStories.title: serde derive on DaemonStatus and HealthIndicator for JSON output
userStories.description: Ensure DaemonStatus and HealthIndicator serialize correctly to JSON. HealthIndicator should serialize as a string ('Green', 'Yellow', 'Red'). Option<u64> fields serialize as null when None.
acceptanceCriteria: DaemonStatus: `#[derive(serde::Serialize)]`
HealthIndicator: `#[derive(Debug, serde::Serialize)]` — serializes as string variant name
`serde_json::to_string_pretty(&status)` on a DaemonStatus with all None fields produces valid JSON
JSON keys use snake_case (default serde behavior)
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: Update README.md and AGENTS.md
userStories.description: Update project documentation to reflect new feature. README.md: add new command to Commands table if applicable, update feature descriptions. AGENTS.md: add new source files to Architecture tree, update Key Patterns if new patterns introduced.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md Commands table includes new command (if a CLI command was added)
README.md describes the new feature/capability
AGENTS.md Architecture src/ tree lists any new .rs files
AGENTS.md Key Dependencies updated if new crates added
userStories.notes: This must be the LAST story executed. Reference README.md and AGENTS.md in project root.
